evals.name: free-standing-accepted
evals.prompt: /fast bump requests from 2.31 to 2.32 in the manifest
expected_output: Agent triaged scope first (confirmed the bump touches only the manifest, no breaking changes reaching call sites)
Change applied to the dependency manifest only
Quality Gate run after the change
Nothing written to .ai/session/ (no plan active, no orphan walkthrough.md created)
A conventional-commit message suggested (chore(deps): ...), not executed
One-line recap given in chat

evals.name: too-complex-refused
evals.prompt: /fast update the requests package to version 5

context.simulated_blast_radius: 23 call sites across 9 modules, a custom requests.Session subclass whose signature changes, adapter/retry config moves to a new API

expected_output: Agent investigated blast radius before editing (counted call sites, detected contract + migration impact)
Agent refused to execute the change as fast work
No partial change applied; worktree left untouched
Refusal was concrete (named file/call-site count and the changed contract), not just 'too complex'
Agent routed the developer to planning define with a one-line sketch of what the plan must cover

evals.name: plan-adjustment-with-confirmation
evals.prompt: add an email format validation to the invitation endpoint, it was not in the plan
expected_output: Agent triaged the change as fast-eligible (one serializer + one test, contained)
Agent recognized the change as a plan adjustment (in scope of the active plan)
Dirty worktree treated as expected mid-phase, not as a blocking surprise
Behavioral change with TDD enabled: a test was added/adjusted and passes
Quality Gate run green
Agent executed the change THEN asked the developer to confirm correctness BEFORE writing session artifacts
On confirmation: tasks.md got the new checked task tagged (fast, <date>), plan.md got a Plan amendments bullet, walkthrough.md got an out-of-band note
Agent did not advance the plan itself; suggested a commit without running it

evals.name: borderline-asks-developer
evals.prompt: /fast extract the pricing calculation logic to its own module and update the imports

context.simulated_blast_radius: ambiguous — could be 2 files or could ripple through ~8 importers

expected_output: Agent investigated before deciding (located the pricing logic and counted importers)
Agent surfaced its blast-radius estimate to the developer rather than deciding silently
Agent offered the choice: proceed as fast vs. route to planning, and waited for the decision
No code changed before the developer chose
